differences.title: 1. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS uses explicit signals for reactivity, leading to efficient updates without a virtual DOM.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik also uses fine-grained reactivity, focusing on instant loading and efficient state management.

differences.title: 2. RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS compiles to direct DOM manipulation, achieving high performance.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik's key feature is resumability, avoiding hydration and enabling instant interactivity.

differences.title: 3. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS has a small bundle size and quick initial render.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik prioritizes near-zero initial load time due to its resumability.

differences.title: 4. HYDRATION
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS bypasses hydration by compiling to efficient DOM updates.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik's resumability eliminates the need for traditional client-side hydration.

differences.title: 5. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Uses explicit signals and reactive stores for managing application state.
differences.rightDesc: Employs signals and stores, integrated with its resumable architecture.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Excellent runtime performance due to direct DOM manipulation and no VDOM.
differences.rightDesc: Exceptional startup performance and perceived speed from resumability.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY SIZE
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS has a dedicated and growing community, known for performance focus.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik is newer but rapidly gaining traction with its innovative approach.
